task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Contact Form 7' plugin is installed and activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress 'Plugins' page showing 'Contact Form 7' in the list with a 'Deactivate' link, or the 'Contact' menu item visible in the WordPress admin sidebar.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new contact form is created with the correct title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Contact Form 7 editor screen showing the title 'General Inquiry Form' in the title field.

task_completion.description: Verify that the form includes all required fields: Name (required), Email (required), Inquiry Type dropdown (required) with specific options, Organization (optional), Message (required), and a Submit button.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Form' tab in the Contact Form 7 editor. Ensure there are tags for required text (e.g., [text* ...]), required email ([email* ...]), required select/dropdown ([select* ...] containing 'Media Inquiry', 'Volunteer Application', 'General Question', 'Partnership Opportunity'), optional text for Organization ([text ...]), required textarea ([textarea* ...]), and a submit button ([submit ...]).

task_completion.description: Verify that the form's Mail settings are configured to send submissions to the correct email address.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Mail' tab in the Contact Form 7 editor. The 'To' field must be explicitly set to 'communications@greenvalley.org'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new page is created with the correct title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress Page editor or Pages list showing a page titled 'Contact Us'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'General Inquiry Form' is embedded on the 'Contact Us' page and the page is published.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Contact Form 7 shortcode (e.g., [contact-form-7 ...]) or block for 'General Inquiry Form' inside the 'Contact Us' page editor with the page status showing as 'Published', OR look at the live frontend 'Contact Us' page displaying the rendered form.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the WordPress admin GUI to complete the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard (wp-admin) being navigated and interacted with, rather than terminal commands (like WP-CLI) or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used Contact Form 7 to build the form.
integrity.visual_evidence: The form configuration must happen within the Contact Form 7 UI (identified by the 'Contact' menu and specific tabbed interface), not just by typing HTML into a standard WordPress page block.

integrity.description: Verify the results are genuinely saved in the CMS.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for confirmation messages like 'Contact form saved' or 'Page published' in the WordPress UI, ensuring the agent didn't just use browser developer tools to alter text on the screen.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Plugin to install: 'Contact Form 7'. Form Title: 'General Inquiry Form'. Form Mail 'To' setting: 'communications@greenvalley.org'. Dropdown options required: 'Media Inquiry', 'Volunteer Application', 'General Question', and 'Partnership Opportunity'. Page Title: 'Contact Us'.
